Details: Oxidation of 17a-ethynyl estradiol (EE2) from water by ultraviolet radiation can be effective depending on the lamp type, dose and contact time. Studies showed that control with UV alone varies between less than 10% and 100%. Complete oxidation was shown in one study only after 120 minutes of UVA exposure. Medium pressure lamps showed more control in comparison to low pressure lamps. The addition of H2O2 or photocatalysis with TiO2 increased the rate of oxidation in comparison to UV alone. Transformation products are unknown.
